subject: Physics
title: How Solar Panels Work
description: A quick win: understand How Solar Panels Work.

hook.text: Solar panels convert sunlight directly into electricity with no moving parts—using the photoelectric effect.

buildup.text: Photons from sunlight hit silicon atoms and knock electrons free, creating an electric current.

discovery.text: A p-n junction in the silicon creates an electric field that pushes freed electrons in one direction—generating usable current.

twist.text: Einstein won his Nobel Prize not for relativity but for explaining the photoelectric effect that makes solar panels work.

climax.text: Solar is now the cheapest form of electricity in history. Costs have dropped 99% since 1976.

punchline.text: Photons knock electrons loose. Current flows. Free energy from sunlight.

quiz.question: What physical effect do solar panels rely on?
